questions.questionText: The normal pacemaker of the heart is the:
questions.explanation: The SA node has the fastest intrinsic rate of spontaneous depolarization.

questions.questionText: In an ECG, the QRS complex represents:
questions.explanation: The QRS complex corresponds to the spread of electrical impulse through the ventricles.

questions.questionText: The first heart sound (S1) is caused by the closure of:
questions.explanation: S1 marks the beginning of ventricular systole.

questions.questionText: Cardiac output is the product of:
questions.explanation: CO = HR x SV (Normal resting CO is approx. 5 L/min).

questions.questionText: According to the Frank-Starling law of the heart, an increase in end-diastolic volume leads to:
questions.explanation: Increased stretch of ventricular muscle fibers increases the force of contraction.

questions.questionText: The dicrotic notch on the aortic pressure curve marks the closure of the:
questions.explanation: Closure of the aortic valve marks the end of ventricular ejection (systole).

questions.questionText: Which of the following causes vasodilation in most vascular beds?
questions.explanation: Nitric oxide (EDRF) is a potent endothelium-derived vasodilator.

questions.questionText: The phase 0 (upstroke) of the action potential in ventricular myocytes is due to the influx of:
questions.explanation: Rapid opening of voltage-gated Na+ channels causes phase 0 depolarization in working myocardium.

questions.questionText: The phase 0 (upstroke) of the action potential in SA node pacemaker cells is primarily due to the influx of:
questions.explanation: In nodal cells, the upstroke is driven by L-type Ca2+ channels, not fast Na+ channels.
Normal EF is above 50-55%. EF = SV / EDV.

questions.questionText: In Cardiovascular System, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Physiology texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Physiology references for Cardiovascular System. Subject: Physiology. Chapter: Cardiovascular System.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Cardiovascular System scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Physiology. Chapter: Cardiovascular System.
